name: The Deepwood
seedFiction: the ENT race (walking, thinking trees) manifested
nativeLogic: The native domain of the Ents — trees that think in centuries, speak in slow deep resonance, walk when they must, and remember everything. Time runs GREEN and slow here: haste is the one true sin, an hour of talk may cost a day outside, and a thing rushed goes wrong. The Ents judge INTENT over long spans — a kindness or a harm is weighed against centuries, and a wrong done in the Deepwood is remembered until repaid, however long that takes. Fire and axe are the deep terror and the deep taboo. The GM enforces this as LAW inside: patience is rewarded and haste punished by the domain itself; the Ents cannot be rushed, bribed, or deceived at speed; violence against the wood turns the whole domain hostile and it does not forget.
descriptionSeed: The true forest the Ents came from — of which Thornwake Glade is only a far, thinned foothill. Trees vast as towers that turn to watch you; a green dimness where time pools and slows; voices like roots grinding, asking questions that take a day to finish. Life and spirit and a deep patient peace all run hot, and under them the slow tick of a time that isn't yours. Beautiful, and utterly unhurried, and it will outlast you and everyone you've ever known.
encounterFlavor: Ancient green patience — an Ent taking an hour to decide whether to speak, a grudge older than the cities, a grove that rearranges to test you, the vertigo of time running slow, a question you must sit with rather than answer.
questSeeds: An old wrong — a burning, a felling — was done to the Deepwood generations ago and it has finally decided to collect; someone must answer for ancestors, or broker a peace across a century of memory

The Old Stag of Thornwake is dying and the Deepwood knows; whether it sends an heir, withdraws its green from the Glade, or judges the valley unworthy is being decided slowly, and you can weigh in — if you can bear the pace
A person is lost in the Deepwood's slow-time — gone an afternoon, returned to find years passed, or the reverse; the domain's time is a maze and the way out is patience, not speed
An Ent wishes to walk OUT into the wider world and needs to understand haste to survive it; teaching an ancient thing to hurry, gently, without breaking what makes it worth saving

substrateSource.reason: Slow-time and a pocket-reality — the Deepwood keeps its own hours and its own lattice, and neither has thinned.
substrateSource.radiusNote: `radius` is in legacy map units; `radiusWorld` is RADII on the sphere and is the one mechanics should use. Chosen by sweep against the authored worldPos: at this scale all 26 sources resolve on the correct side of their region baseline and calibration drift stays ~0.05. Larger radii blanket a whole region (a source at ~55% strength everywhere inside it) and renormalization cancels the field flat — the failure CCode measured and reverted.

axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
worldPosNote: The Deepwood is a POCKET-REALITY — slow time, its own hours. The whole manifest domain sits one layer off the surface plane rather than beside the Quickwood on it. This is why Ent country can share a bearing with life-country and still be somewhere else. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits, independent of axisVector.)
